SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class StringValue

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.StringValue
public final class StringValue extends Object
The StringValue class provides a comprehensive wrapper for managing and manipulating string-based data. It includes features to handle trimmed strings, substrings, comparisons, conversions, alphabetic or numeric checks, data formatting, and concatenation. Also, it supports specific encoding mechanisms such as EBCDIC and ASCII.

This class ensures robust handling of strings in different scenarios, accounting for padding, spaces, and various string manipulations while maintaining immutability where possible.

    Constructor
    Description
    StringValue(byte[] value, boolean ebcdic)
    Constructs a StringValue instance with the specified byte array and encoding type (EBCDIC or ASCII).
    StringValue(String value)
    Constructs a StringValue instance with the specified string value.

    Description

    charAt(int index)
    Returns the character at the specified index within this StringValue instance.

    Compares the current object's value with the specified string lexicographically.

    concat(Object... values)
    Concatenates the string representations of the given input objects, updates the instance's value, and returns the total length of the resulting string.

    Checks whether the specified string is contained within the value of this StringValue instance.

    Counts the total occurrences of specific strings within a base string.

    equals(int value)
    Compares the given integer value, formatted with leading zeroes to match the length of this object's internal value, to determine equality.

    Compares the current string value with the specified NChar field for equality.

    Compares the current string value with the specified NZoned field for equality.

    equals(StringValue value)
    Compares the specified StringValue object with the current StringValue instance for equality.

    equals(String string)
    Compares the current string value with the specified string for equality.

    getString()
    Retrieves the current StringValue instance.
    StringValue
    getString(int offset, int length)
    Extracts a substring from the current StringValue instance starting at a specified offset and spanning the specified length, then returns it as a new StringValue.

    greaterEqualThan(int value)
    Compares the current object with the specified value to determine if it is greater than or equal to the provided value.
    boolean
    greaterEqualThan(AbstractNField field)
    Compares this object with the specified field and determines if the current instance is greater than or equal to the given field.
    boolean
    greaterEqualThan(String value)
    Compares the given value with the current object to determine if it is greater than or equal to the specified value.
    boolean
    greaterThan(int value)
    Compares the given integer value, formatted with leading zeroes to match a specific length, with the current object's formatted value to determine if the current object's value is greater.
    boolean
    greaterThan(AbstractNField field)
    Compares the current object with the specified field to determine if it is greater.
    boolean
    greaterThan(String value)
    Compares this object with the specified value to determine if it is greater.
    boolean
    isAll(byte value)
    Checks whether all bytes in the string value of the current instance are equal to the specified byte value.
    boolean
    isAlphabetic()
    Checks if the string contains only alphabetic characters.
    boolean
    isHighValues()
    Checks whether all bytes in the string value are high values (0xFF).
    boolean
    isLowValues()
    Checks whether all bytes in the string value are low values (0x00).
    boolean
    isNullOrSpaces()
    Checks whether the current string value is either null, composed entirely of spaces, or both.
    boolean
    isNumeric()
    Checks if the given value consists solely of numeric characters.
    boolean
    isSpaces()
    Checks whether all characters in the current string value are spaces (ASCII space character).

    length()
    Calculates and returns the length of the string value stored in this StringValue instance.
    boolean

    Compares the given integer value with the current object's numeric representation and determines if it is less than or equal to the current value.

    Compares the current object with the provided field to determine if it is less than or equal to the specified field.

    lessEqualThan(String value)
    Compares the current object with the specified value to determine if it is less than or equal to the given value.
    boolean
    lessThan(int value)
    Compares the given integer value with the current object's value to determine if the given value is less than the current object's value.
    boolean
    lessThan(AbstractNField field)
    Compares the current object with the specified field and determines whether the current object is less than the specified field.
    boolean
    lessThan(String value)
    Compares the given string value with the current object and determines if it is less than the specified value.
    boolean

    Compares the current object with the specified object for inequality.
    StringValue
    substring(int startIndex, int endIndex)
    Extracts a substring from the current StringValue instance, starting at the specified start index (inclusive) and ending at the specified end index (exclusive).

    Extracts a substring from the current string value that ends at the first occurrence of the specified delimiter.

    Converts the string value stored in this StringValue instance to uppercase and returns the resulting value as a new StringValue instance.

  • Constructor Details

    • StringValue

      public StringValue(String value)
      Constructs a StringValue instance with the specified string value.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to be stored in the instance

    • StringValue

      public StringValue(byte[] value, boolean ebcdic)
      Constructs a StringValue instance with the specified byte array and encoding type (EBCDIC or ASCII).
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array representing the string value
      ebcdic - a boolean flag indicating whether the input byte array is in EBCDIC format

  • Method Details

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • getString

      public StringValue getString()
      Retrieves the current StringValue instance.
      Returns:
      the current instance of StringValue

    • getString

      public StringValue getString(int offset, int length)
      Extracts a substring from the current StringValue instance starting at a specified offset and spanning the specified length, then returns it as a new StringValue.
      Parameters:
      offset - the starting position of the substring, inclusive
      length - the number of characters to include in the substring
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the extracted substring

    • length

      public Integer length()
      Calculates and returns the length of the string value stored in this StringValue instance.
      Returns:
      the number of characters in the string value

    • substring

      public StringValue substring(int startIndex, int endIndex)
      Extracts a substring from the current StringValue instance, starting at the specified start index (inclusive) and ending at the specified end index (exclusive). Returns the extracted substring as a new StringValue instance.
      Parameters:
      startIndex - the starting index of the substring, inclusive
      endIndex - the ending index of the substring, exclusive
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the extracted substring

    • trim

      public StringValue trim()
      Trims any leading and trailing whitespace from the string value stored in this StringValue instance and returns the resulting value as a new StringValue instance.
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the trimmed string value

    • contains

      public boolean contains(String string)
      Checks whether the specified string is contained within the value of this StringValue instance.
      Parameters:
      string - the string to look for within the current string value
      Returns:
      true if the specified string is found, false otherwise

    • charAt

      public char charAt(int index)
      Returns the character at the specified index within this StringValue instance. The index must be within the bounds of the stored string value.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the character to be returned
      Returns:
      the character at the specified index
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is negative or greater than or equal to the length of the string

    • equals

      public boolean equals(StringValue value)
      Compares the specified StringValue object with the current StringValue instance for equality. Equality is determined by comparing the underlying string values of the two objects.
      Parameters:
      value - the StringValue object to compare with the current instance
      Returns:
      true if the underlying string values of the two instances are equal, false otherwise

    • equals

      public boolean equals(String string)
      Compares the current string value with the specified string for equality. The comparison considers partial matches and trailing or leading spaces based on the implementation.
      Parameters:
      string - the string to compare with the current string value
      Returns:
      true if the strings are considered equal based on the defined conditions, false otherwise

    • equals

      public boolean equals(NChar field)
      Compares the current string value with the specified NChar field for equality. Equality is determined based on the underlying string values.
      Parameters:
      field - the NChar field to compare with the current string value
      Returns:
      true if the string value of the current instance is equal to the string value of the specified NChar field; false otherwise

    • equals

      public boolean equals(NZoned field)
      Compares the current string value with the specified NZoned field for equality. Equality is determined based on the string representations of the values.
      Parameters:
      field - the NZoned field to compare with the current string value
      Returns:
      true if the string representation of the current instance is equal to the string representation of the specified NZoned field; false otherwise

    • toUpperCase

      public StringValue toUpperCase()
      Converts the string value stored in this StringValue instance to uppercase and returns the resulting value as a new StringValue instance.
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the uppercase version of the original string value

    • toLowerCase

      public StringValue toLowerCase()
      Converts the string value stored in this StringValue instance to lowercase and returns the resulting value as a new StringValue instance.
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the lowercase version of the original string value

    • isSpaces

      public boolean isSpaces()
      Checks whether all characters in the current string value are spaces (ASCII space character).
      Returns:
      true if all characters in the string value are ASCII spaces, false otherwise

    • isLowValues

      public boolean isLowValues()
      Checks whether all bytes in the string value are low values (0x00).
      Returns:
      true if all bytes in the string value are 0x00, false otherwise

    • isHighValues

      public boolean isHighValues()
      Checks whether all bytes in the string value are high values (0xFF).
      Returns:
      true if all bytes in the string value are 0xFF,

    • isAll

      public boolean isAll(byte value)
      Checks whether all bytes in the string value of the current instance are equal to the specified byte value.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte value to compare against each byte in the string value
      Returns:
      true if all bytes in the string value are equal to the specified byte value, false otherwise

    • isNullOrSpaces

      public boolean isNullOrSpaces()
      Checks whether the current string value is either null, composed entirely of spaces, or both. This method returns true if the string consists solely of low values (0x00) or if all its characters are spaces.
      Returns:
      true if the string value is null, entirely spaces, or low values; false otherwise

    • isNumeric

      public boolean isNumeric()
      Checks if the given value consists solely of numeric characters.
      Returns:
      true if the value contains only numeric characters ('0'-'9'), false otherwise.

    • convertToNumeric

      public BigDecimalValue convertToNumeric()
      Converts the current value to its numeric representation.
      Returns:
      a BigDecimalValue representing the numeric conversion of the current value

    • substringToValue

      public StringValue substringToValue(String delimiter)
      Extracts a substring from the current string value that ends at the first occurrence of the specified delimiter. If the delimiter is not present in the string, the entire string value is returned.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the string to search for within the current string value
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the substring up to (but not including) the first occurrence of the delimiter or, if the delimiter is not found, the entire current string value

    • compare

      public int compare(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current object with the specified AbstractNField object lexicographically.
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNField object to be compared with the current object
      Returns:
      a negative integer if the current object is lexicographically less than the specified object, a positive integer if the current object is lexicographically greater than the specified object, or zero if they are lexicographically equal

    • compare

      public int compare(String value)
      Compares the current object's value with the specified string lexicographically.
      Parameters:
      value - the string to be compared with the current object's value
      Returns:
      a negative integer if the current object's value is lexicographically less than the specified string, a positive integer if it is greater, or zero if both are lexicographically equal

    • greaterEqualThan

      public boolean greaterEqualThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares this object with the specified field and determines if the current instance is greater than or equal to the given field.
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNField object to be compared with this instance.
      Returns:
      true if this instance is greater than or equal to the specified field; false otherwise.

    • greaterThan

      public boolean greaterThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current object with the specified field to determine if it is greater.
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNField object to compare with the current object
      Returns:
      true if the current object is greater than the specified field, false otherwise

    • lessEqualThan

      public boolean lessEqualThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current object with the provided field to determine if it is less than or equal to the specified field.
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNField object to compare against
      Returns:
      true if the current object is less than or equal to the specified field, false otherwise

    • lessThan

      public boolean lessThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current object with the specified field and determines whether the current object is less than the specified field.
      Parameters:
      field - the field to compare with the current object
      Returns:
      true if the current object is less than the specified field, false otherwise

    • greaterEqualThan

      public boolean greaterEqualThan(String value)
      Compares the given value with the current object to determine if it is greater than or equal to the specified value.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to compare with
      Returns:
      true if the current object is greater than or equal to the specified value, false otherwise

    • greaterThan

      public boolean greaterThan(String value)
      Compares this object with the specified value to determine if it is greater.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to be compared with this object
      Returns:
      true if this object is greater than the specified value, false otherwise

    • lessEqualThan

      public boolean lessEqualThan(String value)
      Compares the current object with the specified value to determine if it is less than or equal to the given value.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to compare with the current object
      Returns:
      true if the current object is less than or equal to the specified value, false otherwise

    • lessThan

      public boolean lessThan(String value)
      Compares the given string value with the current object and determines if it is less than the specified value.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to compare against
      Returns:
      true if the current object is less than the specified value, false otherwise

    • equals

      public boolean equals(int value)
      Compares the given integer value, formatted with leading zeroes to match the length of this object's internal value, to determine equality.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to compare with the object's internal value
      Returns:
      true if the formatted string representation of the given value matches the object's internal value; false otherwise

    • notEquals

      public boolean notEquals(int value)
      Compares the current object with the specified object for inequality. This method returns true if the given object is not equal to this object.
      Parameters:
      value - the object to compare for inequality
      Returns:
      true if the specified object is not equal to the current object; false otherwise

    • greaterThan

      public boolean greaterThan(int value)
      Compares the given integer value, formatted with leading zeroes to match a specific length, with the current object's formatted value to determine if the current object's value is greater.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to compare, formatted with leading zeroes to match the length of the current object
      Returns:
      true if the formatted value of the current object is greater than the formatted parameter value, false otherwise

    • lessThan

      public boolean lessThan(int value)
      Compares the given integer value with the current object's value to determine if the given value is less than the current object's value.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to compare
      Returns:
      true if the given value is less than the current object's value; false otherwise

    • greaterEqualThan

      public boolean greaterEqualThan(int value)
      Compares the current object with the specified value to determine if it is greater than or equal to the provided value.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to compare against
      Returns:
      true if the current object is greater than or equal to the specified value, false otherwise

    • lessEqualThan

      public boolean lessEqualThan(int value)
      Compares the given integer value with the current object's numeric representation and determines if it is less than or equal to the current value.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to be formatted and compared
      Returns:
      true if the formatted value is less than or equal to the current value, false otherwise

    • concat

      public int concat(Object... values)
      Concatenates the string representations of the given input objects, updates the instance's value, and returns the total length of the resulting string.
      Parameters:
      values - the objects whose string representations are to be concatenated
      Returns:
      the total length of the concatenated string

    • isAlphabetic

      public boolean isAlphabetic()
      Checks if the string contains only alphabetic characters.
      Returns:
      true if the string consists of alphabetic characters only, otherwise false

    • countStrings

      public int countStrings(Object... strings)
      Counts the total occurrences of specific strings within a base string. The base string is derived from the object's current state.
      Parameters:
      strings - a variable number of objects that are converted to strings and whose occurrences are counted in the base string.
      Returns:
      the total number of occurrences of the specified strings within the base string.
